steps.title: Identify the factoring requirement
steps.content: To factor $x^2 + 3x - 40$, we need two numbers that multiply to $-40$ and add to \$3$.
steps.formula: $$\text{For } x^2 + bx + c, \text{ find } m, n \text{ where } mn = c \text{ and } m + n = b$$

steps.title: Find the pair and write the factorization
steps.content: We need opposite signs in our pair (since the product is $-40$). The pair \$8$ and $-5$ works: $8 + (-5) = 3$ ✓ and $8 \times (-5) = -40$ ✓. The factorization is $(x + 8)(x - 5)$, which we reorder as $(x - 5)(x + 8)$. That matches choice B — B is correct.
